overview:
## Discussion

The system considers an item to be a duplicate for a given keychain when that keychain already has an item of the same class with the same set of composite primary keys. Each class of keychain item has a different set of primary keys, although a few attributes are used in common across all classes. In particular, where applicable, [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrSynchronizable] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrAccessGroup] are part of the set of primary keys. The additional per-class primary keys are listed below:

For generic passwords, the primary keys include [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrAccount] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrService].

For internet passwords, the primary keys include [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrAccount], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrSecurityDomain], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrServer], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrProtocol], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrAuthenticationType], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrPort], and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrPath].

For certificates, the primary keys include [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrCertificateType], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrIssuer], and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrSerialNumber].

For key items, the primary keys include [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeyClass], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeyType], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrApplicationLabel], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrApplicationTag], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeySizeInBits], and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrEffectiveKeySize].

For identity items, which are a certificate and a private key bundled together, the primary keys are the same as for a certificate. Because a private key may be certified more than once, the uniqueness of the certificate determines that of the identity.

# errSecDuplicateItem

**Global Variable**

The item already exists.

## Declaration

```swift
var errSecDuplicateItem: OSStatus { get }
```

## Discussion

The system considers an item to be a duplicate for a given keychain when that keychain already has an item of the same class with the same set of composite primary keys. Each class of keychain item has a different set of primary keys, although a few attributes are used in common across all classes. In particular, where applicable, [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrSynchronizable] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrAccessGroup] are part of the set of primary keys. The additional per-class primary keys are listed below:

For generic passwords, the primary keys include [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrAccount] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrService].

For internet passwords, the primary keys include [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrAccount], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrSecurityDomain], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrServer], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrProtocol], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrAuthenticationType], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrPort], and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrPath].

For certificates, the primary keys include [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrCertificateType], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrIssuer], and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrSerialNumber].

For key items, the primary keys include [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeyClass], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeyType], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrApplicationLabel], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrApplicationTag], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeySizeInBits], and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrEffectiveKeySize].

For identity items, which are a certificate and a private key bundled together, the primary keys are the same as for a certificate. Because a private key may be certified more than once, the uniqueness of the certificate determines that of the identity.
